subject: Biology
title: The Cell Membrane
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Cell Membrane usable.

hook.text: Every cell is wrapped in a membrane just 7 nanometers thick—thinner than a soap bubble.

buildup.text: It's built from a double layer of phospholipids with proteins embedded throughout.

discovery.text: The membrane is fluid—lipids and proteins drift like icebergs on a molecular sea.

twist.text: Without a membrane, a cell's chemistry would dissolve into the surroundings in seconds.

climax.text: This barrier decides what enters and leaves. It separates alive from not alive.

punchline.text: Seven nanometers between life and nothing.

quiz.question: What is the cell membrane primarily made of?
